proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_009 == 1` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than cell lines with `feature_009 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_011 == "skin"` have more negative `dependency_BRAF` scores than non-skin cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_BRAF: mean(`feature_009 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_009 == 1`: mean dependency_BRAF was -0.8047 in the subgroup vs -0.2495 in the complement; signed effect=-0.5552 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_BRAF: mean(`feature_011 == "skin"`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "skin"`: mean dependency_BRAF was -0.4721 in the subgroup vs -0.2938 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1783 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_022 == 1` have more negative `dependency_EGFR` scores than cell lines with `feature_022 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_024 == 1` have more negative `dependency_ERBB2` scores than cell lines with `feature_024 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_EGFR: mean(`feature_022 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_022 == 1`: mean dependency_EGFR was -0.6010 in the subgroup vs -0.2524 in the complement; signed effect=-0.3486 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_ERBB2: mean(`feature_024 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_024 == 1`: mean dependency_ERBB2 was -0.6856 in the subgroup vs -0.2535 in the complement; signed effect=-0.4321 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_014 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than cell lines with `feature_014 == 0`.
Cell lines with `feature_011 == "pancreatic"` have more negative `dependency_KRAS` scores than non-pancreatic cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_027 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RIT1` scores than cell lines with `feature_027 == 0`.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KRAS: mean(`feature_014 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_014 == 1`: mean dependency_KRAS was -0.5594 in the subgroup vs -0.2520 in the complement; signed effect=-0.3075 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KRAS: mean(`feature_011 == "pancreatic"`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "pancreatic"`: mean dependency_KRAS was -0.4260 in the subgroup vs -0.3133 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1127 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_RIT1: mean(`feature_027 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1`: mean dependency_RIT1 was -0.3527 in the subgroup vs -0.2572 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0955 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_POLR2A` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) have more negative `dependency_TMED10` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_POLR2A: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964`: mean dependency_POLR2A was -0.4972 in the subgroup vs -0.3928 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1043 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Univariate linear regression dependency_POLR2A ~ feature_016; effect reported per one-SD increase in feature_016.
analyses.result_summary: A one-SD increase in feature_016 changed dependency_POLR2A by -0.0612 on the dependency-score scale (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_TMED10: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964`: mean dependency_TMED10 was -0.2816 in the subgroup vs -0.2447 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0369 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with both high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) and high `feature_023` (`feature_023 >= 5.669`) have more negative `dependency_DHX9` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 0.428`) and `feature_018 == 1` have more negative `dependency_RPL11` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_DHX9: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964` AND `feature_023 >= 5.669`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964` AND `feature_023 >= 5.669`: mean dependency_DHX9 was -0.3892 in the subgroup vs -0.3297 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0595 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_RPL11: mean(`feature_003 >= 0.428` AND `feature_018 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_003 >= 0.428` AND `feature_018 == 1`: mean dependency_RPL11 was -0.3647 in the subgroup vs -0.2524 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1123 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than non-colorectal cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_035 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than cell lines with `feature_035 == 0`.
Cell lines with high `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.819`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.7204 in the subgroup vs -0.2524 in the complement; signed effect=-0.4680 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_035 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_035 == 1`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.5447 in the subgroup vs -0.2526 in the complement; signed effect=-0.2921 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_028 >= 0.819`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_028 >= 0.819`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.4694 in the subgroup vs -0.2526 in the complement; signed effect=-0.2168 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with both `feature_011 == "colorectal"` and `feature_035 == 1` have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.
Cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_035 == 1`, and high `feature_028` (`feature_028 >= 0.819`) have more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than other cell lines.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.9425 in the subgroup vs -0.2527 in the complement; signed effect=-0.6899 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 0.819`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 0.819`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.1119 in the subgroup vs -0.2530 in the complement; signed effect=-0.8589 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_035 == 1`, `feature_020 == 0`, and `feature_028 >= 1.0` have much more negative `dependency_KIF18A` scores than all other cell lines.

Among cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_035 == 1`, and `feature_028 >= 1.0`, `feature_020 == 1` suppresses `dependency_KIF18A` dependency so scores are less negative than the rest of the dataset.
Among cell lines with `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_035 == 1`, and `feature_020 == 0`, values of `feature_028 < 1.0` do not produce stronger `dependency_KIF18A` dependency.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.3940 in the subgroup vs -0.2530 in the complement; signed effect=-1.1409 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.2534 in the subgroup vs -0.3074 in the complement; signed effect=0.0540 (p=0.0002589).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 < 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 < 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.2738 in the subgroup vs -0.3074 in the complement; signed effect=0.0336 (p=0.004499).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_BRAF` remains `feature_009 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_EGFR` remains `feature_022 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_ERBB2` remains `feature_024 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KRAS` remains `feature_014 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_RIT1` remains `feature_027 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_TMED10` is high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`), with more negative scores than its complement.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, a concentrated subgroup for `dependency_DHX9` is high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`) plus high `feature_023` (`feature_023 >= 5.669`), with more negative scores than its complement.
In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_POLR2A` is high `feature_016` (`feature_016 >= 0.964`), with more negative scores than its complement.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, a concentrated subgroup for `dependency_RPL11` is high `feature_003` (`feature_003 >= 0.428`) plus `feature_018 == 1`, with more negative scores than its complement.

In a systematic dependency-heterogeneity screen, the best-supported subgroup for `dependency_KIF18A` is `feature_011 == "colorectal"`, `feature_035 == 1`, `feature_020 == 0`, and `feature_028 >= 1.0`, with more negative scores than its complement.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_BRAF: mean(`feature_009 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_009 == 1`: mean dependency_BRAF was -0.8047 in the subgroup vs -0.2495 in the complement; signed effect=-0.5552 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_EGFR: mean(`feature_022 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_022 == 1`: mean dependency_EGFR was -0.6010 in the subgroup vs -0.2524 in the complement; signed effect=-0.3486 (p<1e-299).

analyses.result_summary: `feature_024 == 1`: mean dependency_ERBB2 was -0.6856 in the subgroup vs -0.2535 in the complement; signed effect=-0.4321 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KRAS: mean(`feature_014 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_014 == 1`: mean dependency_KRAS was -0.5594 in the subgroup vs -0.2520 in the complement; signed effect=-0.3075 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_RIT1: mean(`feature_027 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_027 == 1`: mean dependency_RIT1 was -0.3527 in the subgroup vs -0.2572 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0955 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_TMED10: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964`: mean dependency_TMED10 was -0.2816 in the subgroup vs -0.2447 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0369 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_DHX9: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964` AND `feature_023 >= 5.669`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964` AND `feature_023 >= 5.669`: mean dependency_DHX9 was -0.3892 in the subgroup vs -0.3297 in the complement; signed effect=-0.0595 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_POLR2A: mean(`feature_016 >= 0.964`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_016 >= 0.964`: mean dependency_POLR2A was -0.4972 in the subgroup vs -0.3928 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1043 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_RPL11: mean(`feature_003 >= 0.428` AND `feature_018 == 1`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_003 >= 0.428` AND `feature_018 == 1`: mean dependency_RPL11 was -0.3647 in the subgroup vs -0.2524 in the complement; signed effect=-0.1123 (p<1e-299).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(`feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -1.3940 in the subgroup vs -0.2530 in the complement; signed effect=-1.1409 (p<1e-299).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The `dependency_KIF18A` complete subgroup requires all four predicates: without `feature_020 == 0` or without `feature_028 >= 1.0`, the subgroup should not show more negative KIF18A dependency.

The final dependency pattern set is heterogeneous: oncogene-like dependencies (`dependency_BRAF`, `dependency_EGFR`, `dependency_ERBB2`, `dependency_KRAS`, `dependency_RIT1`) are mostly single-feature marker effects, whereas `dependency_KIF18A` is a multi-feature colorectal subgroup interaction.

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(absence of `feature_020 == 0`: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: absence of `feature_020 == 0`: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 1` AND `feature_028 >= 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.2534 in the subgroup vs -0.3074 in the complement; signed effect=0.0540 (p=0.0002589).

analyses.code: Welch-style difference test for dependency_KIF18A: mean(absence of high `feature_028`: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 < 1.0`) minus mean(complement).
analyses.result_summary: absence of high `feature_028`: `feature_011 == "colorectal"` AND `feature_035 == 1` AND `feature_020 == 0` AND `feature_028 < 1.0`: mean dependency_KIF18A was -0.2738 in the subgroup vs -0.3074 in the complement; signed effect=0.0336 (p=0.004499).

analyses.code: Compared main-effect marker screens with pair/triple subgroup screens across all dependency outcomes; final KIF18A rule was the only rule requiring four predicates and showing effect magnitude near -1 on the dependency-score scale.
analyses.result_summary: The final screen supported single-marker effects for BRAF, EGFR, ERBB2, KRAS, and RIT1; broader high-feature_016/high-feature_003 programs for POLR2A, TMED10, DHX9, and RPL11; and a distinct four-predicate colorectal KIF18A subgroup with effect -1.1409.
